title: Modular Arithmetic and Public-Key Reasoning · Execution Trace: Crypto Go Fast Exponentiation
summary: An implementation team manipulates big integers without normalizing signs, confuses modular inverse with division, and mistakes a worked toy example for secure key generation. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the fast exponentiation increment in bounded modular arithmetic reasoning notebook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Trace square-and-multiply cost and state why secret exponent code must use vetted constant-time implementations.
Use Euclid and extended Euclid to determine coprimality and modular inverses and identify non-invertible cases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Predict Crypto Go Fast Exponentiation · Theory Crypto Go Fast Exponentiation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 7585 during a certificate rollover incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to normalize modular values and distinguish mathematical correctness from approved constant-time group execution; reject toy, obsolete, unauthenticated, self-signed-trust, downgrade, and security-by-obscurity shortcuts; then double attacker-controlled size or work and remain inside the admission budget. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace square-and-multiply cost and state why secret exponent code must use vetted constant-time implementations.
